profile_backed_target.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-lax-profile-pass32 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- --sample-size 20 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 2

optimization.lever: Same-shape I64 tensor Add fast path in eval_binary_elementwise.
optimization.guard: primitive == Add; both operands are tensors; shapes are equal; both declared dtypes are I64; every element is Literal::I64

after.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-lax-rebench-pass32 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- eval/add_1k_i64_vec --sample-size 60 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3

benchmark.worker_caveat: Baseline and after were both remote RCH measurements on vmi1149989.

isomorphism_proof.ordering_preserved: Yes. The fast path emits one output per pair in the same zipped row-major tensor order as the generic same-shape loop.
isomorphism_proof.dtype_and_shape: Yes. The fast path returns DType::I64 with lhs.shape.clone(), matching promote_dtype(I64, I64) and same-shape generic output shape.
isomorphism_proof.integer_semantics: Identical for public Add. The fast path applies the same int_op closure as the generic binary_literal_op loop; eval_primitive supplies i64::wrapping_add.
isomorphism_proof.malformed_tensor_fallback: Yes. Declared-I64 tensors containing any non-I64 literal return None and fall through to the generic per-element path.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: N/A. The kept path only accepts Literal::I64 elements.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: N/A. Add has no comparisons or ties.
isomorphism_proof.rng: N/A. Add is deterministic and consumes no random state.
isomorphism_proof.scope: Only same-shape I64 tensor Add changes. Scalar, broadcast, mixed dtype, Sub, Mul, Div, complex, F64, and shape-error paths are unchanged.
